text: The new cybersecurity framework requires all federal agencies to implement zero trust architecture by 2027.
Researchers discovered a new species of deep-sea coral in the Pacific Ocean near the Mariana Trench.
Quarterly earnings exceeded analyst expectations driven by strong cloud services and enterprise software growth.
The FDA approved a new once-weekly injection treatment for Type 2 diabetes in adults over 40.
The school district adopted a new STEM curriculum for grades 6 through 12 focused on computational thinking.
Congress passed legislation requiring federal contractors to disclose carbon emissions in annual reports.
Scientists at MIT developed a biodegradable plastic alternative made from seaweed extract.
The Federal Reserve raised interest rates by 25 basis points to combat persistent inflation.
A clinical trial found that a daily aspirin regimen reduced stroke risk by 18% in adults over 65.
The Department of Education released new guidelines for measuring learning outcomes in K-12 schools.
A vulnerability in widely-used open-source cryptography libraries exposed millions of servers to attack.
The Amazon rainforest recorded its driest season in 50 years, threatening biodiversity hotspots.
The city council approved a $2.4 billion public transit expansion connecting suburban neighborhoods to downtown.
Surgeons performed the first fully robotic heart bypass operation at Johns Hopkins Medical Center.
The National Science Foundation awarded $80 million in grants for quantum computing research.
State legislators debated a bill that would require social media platforms to verify user ages.
A new satellite constellation will provide broadband internet to remote and rural communities worldwide.
Global wheat production is expected to decline 7% this year due to drought conditions in major growing regions.
A startup developed an AI-powered tool that detects early-stage pancreatic cancer from blood samples.
The Office of Personnel Management updated telework policies for federal employees after a review period.
Electric vehicle sales surpassed internal combustion engine sales in Norway for the first time in history.
Marine biologists documented a mass coral bleaching event affecting 60% of the Great Barrier Reef.
A pharmaceutical company reported positive Phase 3 trial results for a new Alzheimer's treatment.
The Department of Defense awarded a $12 billion contract for next-generation military communications satellites.
An independent audit found significant cybersecurity gaps in critical infrastructure across the energy sector.
Public school enrollment in rural areas has declined 15% over the past decade as families relocate to cities.
Venture capital investment in climate technology reached a record $70 billion in the past fiscal year.
The CDC updated its guidance on booster vaccinations for immunocompromised adults.
A federal judge blocked the administration's plan to reclassify certain wetlands under the Clean Water Act.
Researchers at Stanford published a paper on using CRISPR to correct genetic mutations causing sickle cell disease.
The company announced a merger that would create the largest semiconductor manufacturer in North America.
Air quality index in several major cities exceeded safe limits for the third consecutive week.
The Transportation Security Administration plans to expand facial recognition technology to 430 airports.
A new study found that bilingual education programs improve cognitive flexibility in elementary school children.
The stock market fell 3% after the jobs report showed unexpected weakness in the manufacturing sector.
The EPA proposed new regulations limiting per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in municipal drinking water.
A community college launched a free coding bootcamp in partnership with local technology employers.
Scientists confirmed that global average sea levels rose 4 millimeters in the last calendar year.
The White House released a national strategy for developing and deploying artificial intelligence responsibly.
Hospitals in rural areas are closing at an accelerating rate due to staffing shortages and financial pressures.
A new programming language designed for high-performance scientific computing gained traction in research labs.
The General Services Administration is piloting a shared services model to consolidate IT infrastructure.
Deforestation rates in Southeast Asia increased 12% last year driven by agricultural expansion.
A randomized controlled trial showed that telemedicine visits were as effective as in-person visits for managing chronic conditions.
The Federal Communications Commission opened a public comment period on proposed net neutrality rules.
An invasive beetle species is threatening ash tree populations across the northeastern United States.
University enrollment in nursing programs increased 22% as healthcare systems struggle to fill open positions.
The Government Accountability Office released a report criticizing delays in the IRS modernization program.
A carbon capture facility in Iceland began storing 4,000 tons of CO2 per year in underground basalt rock.
The National Institutes of Health launched a longitudinal study tracking health outcomes in 100,000 participants over 20 years.
